Fern Porrrrcar PRACTTcEs ConaurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95914-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
March 26,2007
Mark D. Hensley
1230 Rosecrans Avenue, Suite 110
Manhattan Beach, California 90266
RE
Your Request for fnformal Assistance
Our tr'ile No.I-07-046
FACTS
On December 16, 2006, the Commission issued an advice letter at yourrequest,
based on the facts recited in the following paragraphs.
' Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear attitle 2,sections lgl09-
18997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not offer the immunityprovided by a commission opinion or formal written
advice. (Regulation I 8329(c)(3).)
Dear Mr. Hensley:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act').1 since you seek general guldance"rather than advice on
a specific, pending govemmental decision, *" pror,ide you with informal assistanc ;-;- --
QUESTION
If Sony is not the applicant und_e1the facts presented in your prior request for
advice answered in Advice Letter No. I-06-022,wiuldSonybe indirectly involved in
decisions to revise the filming regulations in the El Sagundo Municipal Cod", u. yo,,
described them in your earlier request for advice?
CONCLUSION
Yes. If we assume that Sony does not initiate the proceeding in qrestion, and
further that Sony is neither a named parry_in, nor the subject of the f,ro.""airrg then the
answer to your question is that Sony woul dbe indirectly involved in the decislons you
have described to us.
iry{t


File No. I-07-046
Page No. 2
"on october 17,2006, on its own motion, the El Segundo city council
(consisting of five members) created an adhoc subcommitteeio examine
possible chlnqes to filming regulations set forth in the El segundo
Municipal code. The subcommittee is comprised of two mdbers who
will provide reco,mendationsto the full city council earlynext year
regarding possible changes to the filming regulations.
"The Mayor pro tem, Eric Busch, is emproyed by sony pictures
Entertainment ("Sony') as a corporate safetyrtdunugo. Sony is a
subsidiary o_ruo,"y corporation, which is a Flrtune slo ,o-p*y, kaded
on the New york stock Exchange. In the past year the city irruli tro
filming permits (filming permits are issued adminisnativery, not by the
city council) to Sony for filming projects within the city,s j"ri.aictioo.
"Since Sony is a source of income to Mr. Busch and could be affected by
proposed changes to the ESMC, Mr. Busch recused himself from the
October city council decision, to avoid any potential conflict of interest.
He may, however, desire to participate in iG City Council deliberations
regarding revisions to the city's firming regulations."
On page four of the prior letter, we considered whether Sony was directly or
indirectly involved in the.decisions you described r"garairrg possible revisions to the
filming regulations contained in the city,s Municiprf coa", as follows:
"A business entity that is a source of income is directly involved in a
decision before an official's agency when, directry o, by an agent, it:
"(1) LritiatqF"proceeding in which the decision will be made by
filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar request or;
"(2) Is a named party in, or is the subject o{, the proceeding
conceming the decision before the official or the official's igency. A
person is the subject of a proceeding if a decision invorves the
issuance, renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any licensg
permit, or other entitrement to, or contract with, the subject poroo.
@egulation I S7 M.t (a).)
"If a source of income is not direcfly involved in a governmental decision,
it is treated as indirectly involved therein. (Regulattn rc704.r (b).) w;
do not know at this point whether Sony witi uelirectly or inairectiy
involved in any p_roposed changes to the city's firming regurations, ,o *"
will consider both possibilities in the following analysis.,,


File No. I-07-046
Page No. 3
You now ask us to clarify that sony would be indirectlyinvolved in these
decisions if Sony does not initiaie the proceeding in which the decision will be made.
ANALYSIs
The analysis presented on page forn of the prior letter, quoted above, correctly
states that abusiness entity whichls i ,o.rr." of income is directly involved in a decision
if it initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made, or is a named partyin, or
the subject o{' the proceeding that wilt give rise to the a""i.io.r. Assuming for purposes
of your present question, that sony does not initiate such a proceeding, andfurther that
sony is neither a named party in, nor tr t",:rbj"{-"ith" iroceeding in question, then the
answer to your question is that Sony would be indirectlyilvolved iritrre i"r1.ior* yo,
describe, involving changes to the City,, filming r"goii'tior..
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General CounselfuxM
By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
